item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would this be a joke SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should this be cataloged as a Joke SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great use of Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I missed that. Somewhat amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(17:40) Speech becomes increasingly incoherent.

(17:46) D-45872 has lost all capability of speech.

(16:02) Deterioration of D-45872's mental state has progressed to attempted destruction of objects within the testing room.

(16:07) D-45872 expires.

Hm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't that be 41 rather than 31?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation sources have recently learned of an adult who had consumed SCP 2057 reheated and survived the transformation, dubbed SCP-2057-2. Subject has been functioning within society for several years, with total anonymity. Curiously, subject has exhibited a peculiar memetic effect, with all onlookers excepting 2057-2-1 believing 2057-2 to be a human male, with a high level of respect and expertise within their field.

2057-2-1 refers to one individual in whatever area 2057-2 is in, immune to the memetic effect of 2057-2. When faced with 2057-2, they will attempt to inform others in the same group, with no success. There is no pattern for what typifies 2057-2-1, with individuals being capable of experiencing the behavior and then losing it later within their lives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The former two are just natural consequences of the product - it would logically have to stick skin to skin, or create something that could survive in any area, and if used improperly, it would logically create something dangerous. The latter feels like it wasn't intended for human consumption. This? This is just being a prick. What would be the purpose of turning people into chickens cause they ate cold soup? How do you even come to that?

You know what it is? This is way more Factory than Wondertainment. A seemingly benign, even beneficial product that will fuck you over just cause it can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(17:46) D-45872 has lost all capability of speech. Large white pin feathers cover both of the subject's arms. Smaller feathers have begun sprouting from the subject's face.
(16:02)

Holy crap, this thing can TIME TRAVEL?!!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. It fits too well into the Wondertainment "mold" for me to really enjoy it as a separate item. It's too formulaic, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two good Wondertainments in a row? Hallelujah.

no paint thinner

This is inordinately hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, some of their non-discontinued products have been fairly dangerous when you don't follow the instructions: SCP-1103, implied with SCP-1550, SCP-1916 (heck, the planet X type of that one are dangerous when you do use them according to the instructions…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like a cruel joke made by SCP-261 than a Wondertainment product.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just feels too malicious and nasty to be a Wondertainment item. I get that it's a discontinued one, but it's still worse than it should be. There's no coherent reason it should be turning people into chickens, or worsening symptoms, or inflicting permanent aversions even when it does work. Wondertainment stuff shouldn't be that dangerous to its user, or at least not so obviously so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be also tagged MC&D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a lot of people to thank for helping me refine this to the point of posting. Many people from the chat, including DrStien, Zolgamax, CryogenChaos, TheDeadlyMoose, Voct, derMahler, Djoric, Niight, Djkaktus, thekillerax, Faustos, and many many others. It was a pretty awesome introduction into the world of feedback and editing in real time. Thanks guys! =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted at "a dash of high quality unicorn tears". Wanted to upvote again at the reheating log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point. can fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, yes, chicken soup with a soul.

I liked this in its more bare-bones form, and I like it even more with all this supplemental information. Fundamentally fun, but a little unsettling. Just what a Wondertainment piece should be!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm guessing it has to do with Adventures in Capitalism, but more Wondertainment isn't a bad thing, especially if it's well-written (like Adventures in Capitalism).
One thing I didn't like: the final test log says that D-(random numbers)'s body was 97% covered in feathers. I think it would be more befitting a scientific document saying his/her skin were covered in feathers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said in chat, this is wonderfully silly and I love it. +1 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fully support the influx of Wondertainment-related articles in the past couple weeks.
